skill.name: brand-guidelines
skill.description: Applies Anthropic brand colors, typography guidance, and visual styling rules to documents, presentations, and design artifacts.
skill.summary: Apply Anthropic-style colors, typography, and visual hierarchy to user-facing artifacts.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged URLs, backticks, weak-crypto terms, and reconnaissance terms, but review found only Apache License text and Markdown brand documentation. No executable code, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or runtime network behavior was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Apache License URLs Are Not Runtime Network Access
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs in LICENSE.txt. These URLs appear only in standard Apache License text and do not create network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are Apache License references, not executable code. No evidence found of any request, download, or exfiltration path.

low_findings.title: Apache License Text Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns in LICENSE.txt. The cited lines are license clauses and boilerplate, not cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is legal prose from Apache License 2.0. No hashing, encryption, signing, or credential handling appears on these lines.

low_findings.title: Markdown Color Tokens Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks in SKILL.md as Ruby or shell execution. The backticks wrap static hex color values used for brand styling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown inline-code formatting around color constants. There is no script block, interpreter directive, or command invocation.

low_findings.title: Brand Documentation Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md prose as weak cryptography. The cited lines describe brand usage, keywords, and font fallback behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is descriptive Markdown content, not implementation. No cryptographic primitive or security-sensitive operation is present.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Pattern Matches Are Benign Terms
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. The cited lines contain a color name and a reference to python-pptx RGBColor usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 23 is a brand color entry, and line 72 names a color helper class. No evidence found of host, network, or environment enumeration.

content.value_statement: Brand styling can become inconsistent across documents, slides, and interface mockups. This skill gives clear Anthropic-style color and typography guidance for polished visual artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Lists Anthropic-style main colors and accent colors with hex values.
Recommends Poppins for headings and Lora for body text.
Defines fallback fonts for environments without custom fonts.
Explains text hierarchy rules for headings and body copy.
Guides use of accent colors for non-text shapes.
References RGB color application for precise visual matching.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automated transformation tools.
Does not bundle Poppins, Lora, or other font files.
Does not provide legal permission to use Anthropic trademarks.
Does not include templates, images, logos, or downloadable brand assets.

use_cases.title: Polish a Presentation
use_cases.description: Apply consistent colors, heading fonts, body fonts, and accent styling to a slide deck.

use_cases.title: Style a Document
use_cases.description: Refresh a report or proposal with clear visual hierarchy and Anthropic-style typography.

use_cases.title: Align a UI Mockup
use_cases.description: Use the listed palette and typography rules while designing brand-aligned interface screens.

prompt_templates.title: Style a Simple Artifact
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small document or slide.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the brand-guidelines skill to this artifact. Use the documented colors, typography, and readable fallbacks.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh a Slide Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this slide deck and update the visual style using the brand-guidelines skill. Preserve the message, improve hierarchy, and use accent colors sparingly.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Brand-Aligned Layout
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a new visual artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a new one-page layout using the brand-guidelines skill. Apply the color palette, heading and body font rules, and accessible contrast.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Visual Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing design system or artifact set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these artifacts against the brand-guidelines skill. Identify inconsistent colors, typography issues, weak hierarchy, and specific changes to make.

output_examples.input: A plain internal strategy slide with mixed fonts and several unrelated accent colors.
output_examples.output: The slide uses dark primary text, a light background, Poppins-style headings, Lora-style body copy, and one orange accent for emphasis.

output_examples.input: A product brief that needs a stronger branded visual hierarchy.
output_examples.output: The brief receives clearer heading levels, consistent body typography, restrained gray supporting elements, and brand-matched accent treatment.

output_examples.input: A dashboard mockup that should feel closer to Anthropic visual styling.
output_examples.output: The mockup uses the documented palette, readable fallback fonts, and consistent color roles for text, surfaces, and non-text shapes.

best_practices: Use the accent colors for emphasis, not for large areas of dense text.
Keep heading and body typography consistent across the whole artifact.
Check contrast after applying brand colors, especially on dark backgrounds.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the palette as permission to use protected trademarks or logos.
Do not mix many accent colors in the same small section.
Do not assume custom fonts are installed without defining fallbacks.

faq.question: Does this skill include official Anthropic logo files?
faq.answer: No. It provides color and typography guidance only.

faq.question: Can it automatically edit files by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains guidance, not executable automation.

faq.question: Which fonts does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends Poppins for headings and Lora for body text, with common fallback fonts.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this a complete brand system?
faq.answer: No. It is a compact styling guide for colors, typography, and basic visual hierarchy.

faq.question: Does it create legal approval for brand use?
faq.answer: No. Users must confirm trademark and brand permission separately.
